rp_a1.question_text: Is there a general prohibition on dividends/distributions?
rp_a1.description: Whether the agreement contains a baseline dividend prohibition

rp_a2.question_text: Which entities does the dividend covenant apply to?
rp_a2.description: Select all entities bound by the dividend restriction

rp_a3.question_text: What actions are included in the definition of 'Dividend'?
rp_a3.description: Select all actions that constitute a restricted dividend

rp_b1.question_text: Which entities may receive unrestricted intercompany dividends?
rp_b1.description: Select all permitted recipients of upstream dividends

rp_c1.question_text: Is there a management equity repurchase basket?
rp_c1.description: Whether equity repurchases from management/employees are permitted

rp_c2.question_text: Which persons are covered by the management equity basket?
rp_c2.description: Select all persons whose equity can be repurchased

rp_c3.question_text: Which events trigger management equity repurchase rights?
rp_c3.description: Select all events permitting equity repurchase

rp_c4.question_text: What is the annual dollar cap on management equity repurchases?

rp_c5.question_text: What is the EBITDA-based annual cap percentage?
rp_c5.description: Cap as percentage of Consolidated EBITDA

rp_c6.question_text: Is the cap the greater of dollar amount and EBITDA percentage?
rp_c6.description: Whether grower mechanic applies to management equity cap

rp_c7.question_text: Is unused annual capacity carried forward to future years?
rp_c7.description: Whether unused management equity basket rolls over

rp_d1.question_text: Is there a tax distribution basket?
rp_d1.description: Whether distributions for tax obligations are permitted

rp_d2.question_text: Which types of tax groups are covered?
rp_d2.description: Select all tax group types for which distributions are permitted

rp_d3.question_text: Is distribution capped at standalone corporate taxpayer amount?
rp_d3.description: Whether tax distributions are limited to standalone calculation

rp_e1.question_text: Which equity award types permit deemed repurchases for tax withholding?
rp_e1.description: Select all equity award types covered

rp_f1.question_text: Is there a builder basket / Cumulative Amount?

rp_f2.question_text: What is the builder basket starter amount in USD?

rp_f3.question_text: What is the EBITDA-based starter amount percentage?
rp_f3.description: Starter as percentage of Consolidated EBITDA

rp_f4.question_text: Is the starter the greater of dollar and EBITDA percentage?
rp_f4.description: Whether grower mechanic applies to builder starter

rp_f5.question_text: What percentage of CNI builds into the basket?
rp_f5.description: Percentage of Consolidated Net Income that builds capacity

rp_f6.question_text: What percentage of equity proceeds builds into the basket?
rp_f6.description: Percentage of qualified equity issuance proceeds

rp_f7.question_text: Which sources build into the Cumulative Amount?
rp_f7.description: Select all sources that increase builder capacity

rp_f8.question_text: Which uses reduce the Cumulative Amount?
rp_f8.description: Select all uses that decrease builder capacity

rp_g1.question_text: Is there a ratio-based unlimited dividend basket?
rp_g1.description: Whether unlimited dividends are permitted at a leverage threshold

rp_g2.question_text: What is the First Lien Leverage Ratio threshold at or below which unlimited restricted payments are permitted on a pro forma basis?
rp_g2.description: First Lien or Total leverage ratio threshold

rp_g3.question_text: What is the interest coverage ratio threshold?
rp_g3.description: FCCR or interest coverage threshold if applicable

rp_g4.question_text: Are dividends truly unlimited if ratio is met?
rp_g4.description: Whether there's any cap once the ratio test passes

rp_h1.question_text: Which holding company overhead costs may be funded by dividends?
rp_h1.description: Select all permitted overhead expense types

rp_h2.question_text: Which transaction costs may be funded by dividends?

rp_i1.question_text: Which baskets are reduced when amounts are reallocated away?
rp_i1.description: Select baskets subject to reduction from reallocation

rp_i2.question_text: Which baskets can receive reallocated amounts?
rp_i2.description: Select baskets that can receive reallocated capacity

rp_j1.description: Whether borrower can designate subsidiaries as unrestricted

rp_j2.question_text: What percentage of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period is the grower component of the general RP basket (e.g., 100% means the basket is the greater of the dollar amount and 100% of EBITDA)?
rp_j2.description: Whether unrestricted designation is blocked during default

rp_j3.description: Whether board approval is required for unrestricted designation

rp_j4.question_text: What is the dollar cap on investments in unrestricted subs?
rp_j4.description: Maximum investment amount in unrestricted subsidiaries

rp_j5.question_text: What is the percentage of total assets cap?
rp_j5.description: Maximum as percentage of consolidated total assets

rp_k1.question_text: Does the agreement contain a J.Crew blocker?
rp_k1.description: Provision restricting IP/asset transfers to unrestricted subs

rp_k2.question_text: Does the blocker cover intellectual property?
rp_k2.description: Whether IP transfers to unrestricted subs are blocked

rp_k3.question_text: Does the blocker cover material assets?
rp_k3.description: Whether material asset transfers are blocked

rp_k4.question_text: Which entities are bound by the J.Crew blocker?
rp_k4.description: Select all entities subject to the blocker restriction

rp_k5.question_text: When does the J.Crew blocker apply?
rp_k5.description: Select all triggering events for the blocker

rp_k6.question_text: Which types of IP are included in the blocker definition?
rp_k6.description: Select all IP types covered by the blocker

rp_k7.question_text: Which types of transfers are restricted by the blocker?

rp_k8.question_text: Is the J.Crew blocker a sacred right (requires all lender consent)?
rp_k8.description: Whether amendment requires affected/all lender consent

rp_s1.question_text: Which types of payments constitute Restricted Debt Payments?
rp_s1.description: Select all payment types subject to the RDP restriction

rp_t1.question_text: Which baskets are available for Restricted Debt Payments?

rp_z1.description: Combination of weak blocker + permissive unrestricted sub rules

rp_z2.description: Weak pro rata sharing or open market purchase provisions

rp_z3.description: Weak asset transfer restrictions combined with unrestricted sub flexibility

rp_f9.question_text: What are ALL the sources that build the Cumulative Amount? Select all that apply.
rp_f9.extraction_prompt: Identify every source that adds to the Cumulative Amount or Available Amount basket. Check each: starter amount, CNI percentage, ECF (Excess Cash Flow), EBITDA minus Fixed Charges, equity proceeds, debt conversion proceeds, returns on investments, retained asset sale proceeds, declined proceeds, other.

rp_f10.question_text: Is Excess Cash Flow (ECF) a build source for the Cumulative Amount?
rp_f10.extraction_prompt: Determine if Excess Cash Flow that is not required to prepay debt builds the Cumulative Amount. Look for language like 'Excess Cash Flow not applied to mandatory prepayment' or similar.

rp_f11.question_text: What is the ECF-based builder formula (if applicable)?
rp_f11.extraction_prompt: If ECF builds the Cumulative Amount, extract the exact formula. Include any floors (e.g., 'cannot be less than $0'), percentage retained, and conditions.

rp_f12.question_text: Is there an EBITDA minus Fixed Charges test as a build source?
rp_f12.extraction_prompt: Determine if cumulative EBITDA minus a multiple of Fixed Charges is a build source. Look for formulas like 'EBITDA minus 140% of Consolidated Fixed Charges' or similar.

rp_f13.question_text: What is the Fixed Charges multiplier in the EBITDA test (e.g., 140%)?
rp_f13.extraction_prompt: If an EBITDA minus Fixed Charges test exists, what multiple of Fixed Charges is subtracted? Common values: 100%, 125%, 140%, 150%.

rp_f14.question_text: Does the Cumulative Amount use 'greatest of' multiple alternative tests?
rp_f14.extraction_prompt: Determine if the Cumulative Amount is calculated as the GREATEST OF multiple alternative formulas (e.g., greatest of CNI test, ECF test, and EBITDA test), rather than a sum of components.

rp_f15.question_text: What is the exact start date for Cumulative Amount accumulation?
rp_f15.extraction_prompt: Extract the VERBATIM language describing when the Cumulative Amount starts accumulating. Examples: 'the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs', 'the Closing Date', 'the first full fiscal quarter after the Closing Date'.

rp_f16.question_text: Do asset sale proceeds that are not required for mandatory debt prepayment (Retained Asset Sale Proceeds) build the Cumulative Amount, creating a pathway from asset sales to dividend capacity via the builder basket?
rp_f16.extraction_prompt: Determine if asset sale proceeds that are not required to be applied to prepayment (Retained Asset Sale Proceeds, Declined Proceeds) are added to the Cumulative Amount.

rp_f17.question_text: Do returns/distributions from Investments build the Cumulative Amount?
rp_f17.extraction_prompt: Determine if cash returns, profits, distributions received from Investments (including from Unrestricted Subsidiaries) are added to the Cumulative Amount.

rp_g5.question_text: Does the ratio basket include a 'no worse' test that permits the transaction even if leverage exceeds the absolute threshold, as long as pro forma leverage is no worse than immediately before the transaction?
rp_g5.extraction_prompt: Determine if dividends are permitted when the leverage ratio is 'no worse' than before giving effect to the dividend, even if above the normal threshold. Look for language like 'if the ratio would not be worse on a pro forma basis'.

rp_g6.question_text: Is the no-worse test uncapped, meaning any leverage level is permitted as long as pro forma leverage does not exceed the pre-transaction level? If capped, what is the maximum leverage ratio permitted under the no-worse test?
rp_g6.extraction_prompt: If a 'no worse' test exists at a different threshold than the unlimited ratio basket, what is that threshold? For example, unlimited at 3.50x but 'no worse' permitted up to 6.25x.

rp_g7.question_text: Are there multiple leverage tiers with different dividend permissions?
rp_g7.extraction_prompt: Determine if there are multiple ratio thresholds that permit different levels of dividends. For example: unlimited below 3.50x, capped amount between 3.50x-4.50x, prohibited above 4.50x.

rp_i7.question_text: Can amounts from other baskets be reallocated to make Restricted Payments?
rp_i7.extraction_prompt: Determine if the RP covenant permits using capacity from other baskets (Investment basket, RDP basket, prepayment basket) to make Restricted Payments. Look for cross-references like 'amounts available under Section 6.03(y)' or 'amounts available under Section 6.09(a)'.

rp_i8.question_text: Which baskets can be reallocated to Restricted Payments?
rp_i8.extraction_prompt: Identify ALL baskets whose capacity can be used for Restricted Payments. Common sources: Investment basket (6.03), Restricted Debt Payment basket (6.09), prepayment basket, intercompany debt basket.

rp_i3.question_text: Can amounts available for restricted debt payments be reallocated dollar-for-dollar to make dividends, adding to total quantifiable dividend capacity under the general RP basket?
rp_i3.extraction_prompt: Identify the specific section that permits reallocation from other baskets to Restricted Payments. Usually a lettered subsection like 6.06(j) or 6.06(k).

rp_i4.question_text: What is the dollar amount of restricted debt payment capacity that can be reallocated to make dividends, adding to the general RP basket's dividend capacity?
rp_i4.extraction_prompt: If the RDP basket (Section 6.09) can be reallocated to Restricted Payments, what is the dollar amount of that basket? Look for the general RDP basket amount, often structured as 'greater of $X and Y% of EBITDA'.

rp_i5.question_text: Can amounts available for permitted investments be reallocated dollar-for-dollar to make dividends, adding to total quantifiable dividend capacity under the general RP basket?
rp_i5.extraction_prompt: If the Investment basket (Section 6.03) can be reallocated to Restricted Payments, what is the dollar amount? Look for the general Investment basket, often structured as 'greater of $X and Y% of EBITDA' or 'greater of $X and Y% of Total Assets'.

rp_i6.question_text: What is the dollar amount of investment capacity that can be reallocated to make dividends, adding to the general RP basket's dividend capacity?
rp_i6.extraction_prompt: Determine if the Restricted Payment basket capacity can also be used for Investments or Restricted Debt Payments, not just the reverse.

rp_l1.question_text: Can asset sale proceeds ultimately be used for dividends?
rp_l1.extraction_prompt: Determine if Net Cash Proceeds from asset sales can build the Cumulative Amount or otherwise be used for Restricted Payments. This can occur through Retained Asset Sale Proceeds, Declined Proceeds, or proceeds not subject to mandatory prepayment.

rp_l2.question_text: Are there leverage-based tiers for asset sale sweep percentages?
rp_l2.extraction_prompt: Determine if the mandatory prepayment of asset sale proceeds varies by leverage ratio. For example: 50% sweep above 5.75x, 0% sweep below 5.50x.

rp_l3.question_text: What is the first leverage tier and sweep percentage for asset sales?
rp_l3.extraction_prompt: Extract the first/highest leverage tier and its sweep percentage. Format as: '[ratio]x = [percentage]% sweep'. Example: '5.75x = 50% sweep'.

rp_l4.question_text: What is the second leverage tier and sweep percentage for asset sales?
rp_l4.extraction_prompt: Extract the second leverage tier and its sweep percentage if it exists. Format as: '[ratio]x = [percentage]% sweep'. Example: '5.50x = 0% sweep'.

rp_l5.question_text: What is the de minimis threshold for individual asset sales (below which no sweep)?
rp_l5.extraction_prompt: Identify the dollar threshold below which individual asset sales are not subject to mandatory prepayment. Often structured as 'greater of $X and Y% of EBITDA'.

rp_l6.question_text: What is the annual de minimis threshold for asset sale sweeps?
rp_l6.extraction_prompt: Identify the annual aggregate threshold below which asset sales are not swept. Often structured as 'greater of $X and Y% of EBITDA per fiscal year'.

rp_l7.question_text: Which types of asset sales are exempt from mandatory prepayment?
rp_l7.extraction_prompt: Identify ALL categories of asset sales exempt from sweep. Common exemptions: non-Collateral assets, ordinary course sales, Casualty Events, sales under de minimis threshold, intercompany sales, sale-leasebacks.

rp_l8.question_text: Can asset sales under the ratio basket (unlimited basket) avoid sweep?
rp_l8.extraction_prompt: Determine if asset sales made under a ratio-based unlimited basket (often Section 6.05(z)) are exempt from mandatory prepayment. Look for cross-references between asset sale covenant and prepayment provisions.

rp_l9.question_text: What ratio must be met for asset sale proceeds to avoid sweep?
rp_l9.extraction_prompt: If a ratio test exempts asset sale proceeds from sweep, what is the threshold? For example, 'if First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 6.25x or less'.

rp_n1.question_text: What is the general/catch-all Restricted Payment basket amount?
rp_n1.extraction_prompt: Identify the general RP basket that can be used for any Restricted Payment. Often structured as 'greater of $X and Y% of EBITDA' or 'greater of $X and Y% of Total Assets'.

rp_n2.question_text: What is the grower percentage for the general RP basket?
rp_n2.extraction_prompt: If the general RP basket uses a 'greater of' formula, what is the EBITDA or Total Assets percentage? For example, '100% of EBITDA' or '1.5% of Total Assets'.

rp_n3.question_text: List ALL quantifiable RP baskets with their dollar amounts.
rp_n3.extraction_prompt: Create a comprehensive list of every Restricted Payment basket that has a quantifiable dollar amount. Format each as: 'Basket name: $X (or greater of $X / Y% EBITDA)'. Include: starter amount, general basket, management equity, tax distributions, specific purpose baskets.

rp_n4.question_text: Which RP baskets require a ratio test to be met?
rp_n4.extraction_prompt: Identify which Restricted Payment baskets require leverage ratio compliance. Common: ratio unlimited basket, builder basket usage, certain specific baskets.

rp_n5.question_text: Which RP baskets require no Event of Default?
rp_n5.extraction_prompt: Identify which Restricted Payment baskets require that no Event of Default exists. Some baskets may be available even during a default.

rp_k9.question_text: Does the J.Crew blocker restrict transfer of Material Intellectual Property to unrestricted subsidiaries at the point of designation? (This restriction governs what can be moved INTO an unrestricted subsidiary, not distribution of equity in already-designated unrestricted subsidiaries.)
rp_k9.extraction_prompt: Determine if the J.Crew blocker restricts TRANSFER of Material IP to unrestricted subsidiaries. This is about moving assets INTO an unsub at the point of designation, NOT about distributing equity of an already-designated unsub. Look for language like 'shall not transfer, sell, or otherwise dispose of Material Intellectual Property to any Unrestricted Subsidiary'.

rp_k10.question_text: Does the J.Crew blocker restrict designation of a subsidiary as unrestricted if it would own or hold an exclusive license to Material Intellectual Property? (This restriction applies at the moment of designation, not to subsequent distributions from already-designated unrestricted subsidiaries.)
rp_k10.extraction_prompt: Determine if the J.Crew blocker prevents DESIGNATION of a subsidiary as unrestricted if that subsidiary owns or holds an exclusive license to Material IP. This is a condition on the act of designation itself, not on later distributions. Look for language like 'shall not designate any subsidiary as an Unrestricted Subsidiary if such subsidiary owns or holds an exclusive license to Material Intellectual Property'.

rp_m1.question_text: Are distributions received from Unrestricted Subsidiaries carved out from the Restricted Payment prohibition?
rp_m1.extraction_prompt: Determine if the RP covenant contains a specific carve-out permitting receipt of dividends, distributions, or other payments from Unrestricted Subsidiaries without counting as a Restricted Payment. This may appear as a separate permitted basket or as an exclusion from the definition of 'Restricted Payment'.

rp_m2.question_text: Do cash distributions received from Unrestricted Subsidiaries rebuild the Cumulative Amount / builder basket?
rp_m2.extraction_prompt: Check if the Cumulative Amount / Available Amount definition includes 'cash dividends received from Unrestricted Subsidiaries' or 'cash distributions received from Unrestricted Subsidiaries' as a component that increases the builder basket. This is typically in the builder basket definition alongside JV returns.

rp_m3.question_text: Are non-cash distributions from Unrestricted Subsidiaries included (at fair market value)?
rp_m3.extraction_prompt: Determine if the builder basket or carve-out for Unrestricted Subsidiary distributions extends beyond cash to include fair market value of non-cash dividends, property distributions, or in-kind distributions. Some agreements limit to cash only; others include non-cash at FMV.

rp_m4.question_text: Do distributions received from Unrestricted Subsidiaries reduce the Investment basket that was used to fund the original investment?
rp_m4.extraction_prompt: Determine if receiving distributions from an Unrestricted Subsidiary causes a dollar-for-dollar (or other) reduction in the Investment basket capacity under Section 6.03. Some agreements treat the return as reducing the original Investment; others do not, effectively allowing double-counting.

rp_m5.question_text: Is there a cap on the amount of Unrestricted Subsidiary distributions that can re-enter the restricted group or rebuild the builder basket?
rp_m5.extraction_prompt: Check if there is a dollar cap, percentage cap, or 'not to exceed the original Investment amount' limitation on how much of the distributions received from Unrestricted Subsidiaries can build into the Cumulative Amount or otherwise benefit the restricted group. An uncapped carve-out is more borrower-friendly.

rp_m6.question_text: If capped, what is the cap on Unrestricted Subsidiary distributions that rebuild the builder basket?
rp_m6.extraction_prompt: If there is a limitation, extract the cap formula. Common patterns: 'not to exceed the aggregate amount of Investments made in such Unrestricted Subsidiary', 'limited to the lesser of (x) and (y)', or a fixed dollar amount. Return the verbatim cap language.

rp_m7.question_text: Is redesignation of an Unrestricted Subsidiary back to restricted status treated as a return of the original Investment (rebuilding capacity)?
rp_m7.extraction_prompt: Determine if redesignating an Unrestricted Subsidiary back to Restricted Subsidiary status causes the fair market value (or lesser of FMV and original Investment) to be added back to the Cumulative Amount or Investment basket capacity. This is the 'redesignation credit' mechanic.

rp_m8.question_text: For redesignation credit, is the amount the lesser of fair market value and the original Investment amount, or just FMV?
rp_m8.extraction_prompt: When an Unrestricted Subsidiary is redesignated back to restricted, what value is credited: (a) fair market value at time of redesignation, (b) lesser of FMV and original Investment amount, or (c) original Investment amount. Option (a) is most borrower-friendly as the unsub may have appreciated. Extract the specific formula.

rp_m9.question_text: Can proceeds from Unrestricted Subsidiary distributions be used as the basis for further Restricted Payments (cascading)?
rp_m9.extraction_prompt: Determine if there is any anti-cascading or anti-duplication language preventing distributions received from Unrestricted Subsidiaries from being used to fund new Restricted Payments. Without such language, the borrower can: (1) invest in unsub, (2) receive distribution back (rebuilding builder), (3) use rebuilt capacity for dividends — effectively laundering restricted payment capacity through an unrestricted subsidiary.

rp_m10.question_text: Is there anti-duplication language preventing the same Unrestricted Subsidiary distribution from being counted in multiple baskets simultaneously?
rp_m10.extraction_prompt: Check for 'without duplication' or similar language ensuring that the same distribution from an Unrestricted Subsidiary is not counted both as a builder basket increase AND as a separate permitted RP carve-out. Anti-duplication prevents double-counting of the same cash flow.

rp_p1.question_text: Is there a specific carve-out permitting distribution of equity interests, indebtedness, and assets of already-designated unrestricted subsidiaries, independent of the J.Crew blocker and without any dollar cap or ratio test?
rp_p1.extraction_prompt: Determine if there is a specific permitted Restricted Payment basket or carve-out that allows distribution of equity interests, indebtedness, or assets of Unrestricted Subsidiaries. This is separate from the J.Crew blocker and separate from the general unsub distribution rebuild mechanics. Look for language like 'distributions of shares of Equity Interests in, or Indebtedness or other obligations of, or other assets constituting Investments in, Unrestricted Subsidiaries'.

rp_p2.question_text: Does the unrestricted subsidiary distribution carve-out cover shares of equity interests in unrestricted subsidiaries?
rp_p2.extraction_prompt: Within the unsub distribution carve-out, determine if it covers distribution of shares of Equity Interests in Unrestricted Subsidiaries. Look for 'shares of Equity Interests in ... Unrestricted Subsidiaries' or similar language.

rp_p3.question_text: Does the unrestricted subsidiary distribution carve-out cover distribution of any assets of unrestricted subsidiaries and the proceeds thereof?
rp_p3.extraction_prompt: Within the unsub distribution carve-out, determine if it covers distribution of assets of Unrestricted Subsidiaries and the proceeds thereof. Look for 'other assets constituting Investments in Unrestricted Subsidiaries' or 'proceeds thereof'.

rp_p4.question_text: Is the unrestricted subsidiary distribution carve-out categorical, meaning it permits distribution without any dollar cap, leverage test, or ratio condition?
rp_p4.extraction_prompt: Determine if the unsub distribution carve-out is unconditional — i.e., no dollar cap, no leverage ratio test, no pro forma condition, no basket limit. A categorical carve-out means any amount can be distributed from already-designated unrestricted subsidiaries without restriction. If there are ANY conditions, answer false and note the conditions.

rp_g8.question_text: What ratio type is used for the unlimited dividend basket?
rp_g8.description: First lien, total, secured, or net leverage ratio

rp_g9.question_text: Is the ratio tested on a pro forma basis?
rp_g9.description: Whether leverage is measured after giving effect to the dividend

rp_g10.description: Declaration date, payment date, or most recent fiscal quarter

rp_g11.question_text: Is Limited Condition Transaction treatment available for the ratio basket?
rp_g11.description: Whether LCT election freezes the test date for ratio compliance

rp_g12.question_text: What is the no-worse leverage threshold?
rp_g12.description: Maximum ratio permitted under the no-worse test if different from unlimited threshold

rp_h3.question_text: Is there a holdco overhead basket permitting dividends for holding company expenses?
rp_h3.description: Whether a specific basket exists for holdco overhead distributions

rp_h4.question_text: What is the annual cap on holdco overhead distributions?
rp_h4.description: Maximum annual amount in USD for holding company expenses

rp_h5.question_text: Does the holdco overhead basket cover management or advisory fees?
rp_h5.description: Whether management fees paid to sponsors or advisors are included

rp_h6.question_text: Does the holdco overhead basket cover administrative expenses?
rp_h6.description: Whether general administrative and corporate expenses are included

rp_h7.question_text: Does the holdco overhead basket cover franchise taxes?
rp_h7.description: Whether state franchise taxes and similar taxes are included

rp_h8.question_text: Does the holdco overhead basket require arm's length terms?
rp_h8.description: Whether overhead fees must be on arm's length or market terms

rp_h9.question_text: Does the holdco overhead basket require board approval?
rp_h9.description: Whether board resolution is needed for holdco overhead distributions

rp_h10.question_text: Does the holdco overhead basket cover insurance premiums?
rp_h10.description: Whether D&O or other insurance premiums are included

rp_h11.question_text: Does the holdco overhead basket cover director fees?
rp_h11.description: Whether board of directors compensation is included

rp_h12.question_text: Does the holdco overhead basket cover audit or accounting fees?
rp_h12.description: Whether audit, accounting, and financial reporting costs are included

rp_h13.question_text: Does the holdco overhead basket cover legal fees?
rp_h13.description: Whether legal counsel and compliance costs are included

rp_h14.question_text: What is the scope of management fee recipients?
rp_h14.description: Whether management fees are limited to sponsor or include broader affiliates

rp_e2.question_text: Is there an equity award basket permitting deemed repurchases?
rp_e2.description: Whether a specific basket exists for equity award related repurchases

rp_e3.question_text: What is the annual cap on equity award repurchases?
rp_e3.description: Maximum annual amount in USD for equity award basket

rp_e4.question_text: Is unused equity award basket capacity carried forward?
rp_e4.description: Whether unused annual equity award capacity rolls over

rp_e5.question_text: Does the equity award basket cover cashless exercise?
rp_e5.description: Whether cashless exercise of options counts as a permitted repurchase

rp_e6.question_text: Does the equity award basket cover tax withholding?
rp_e6.description: Whether deemed repurchases for tax withholding obligations are covered

rp_e7.question_text: Does the equity award basket cover net settlement?
rp_e7.description: Whether net settlement of equity awards is a permitted repurchase

rp_e8.question_text: Does the equity award basket cover clawback provisions?
rp_e8.description: Whether equity clawbacks are treated as permitted repurchases

rp_e9.question_text: Does the equity award basket cover forfeiture?
rp_e9.description: Whether equity forfeitures are treated as permitted repurchases

rp_c8.question_text: What is the maximum carryforward period in years?
rp_c8.description: Number of years unused management equity capacity can carry forward

rp_c9.question_text: What is the eligible person scope for the management equity basket?
rp_c9.description: Whether limited to current employees or includes former employees, family members, etc.

rp_d4.question_text: What is the hypothetical tax rate used for tax distributions?
rp_d4.description: The assumed combined rate for calculating tax distribution amounts

rp_d5.question_text: Are tax sharing agreement payments permitted?
rp_d5.description: Whether distributions under tax sharing agreements are permitted

rp_d6.description: Whether distributions for estimated or quarterly tax payments are covered

rp_f18.question_text: Does usage of builder basket capacity for Restricted Payments reduce this basket's own remaining balance? (Per-basket usage accounting — does not indicate shared capacity with other RP baskets.)
rp_f18.description: Whether RP draws reduce the Cumulative Amount

rp_f19.question_text: Does usage of builder basket capacity for Restricted Debt Payments reduce this basket's own remaining balance? (Per-basket usage accounting — does not indicate shared capacity with other RDP baskets.)
rp_f19.description: Whether RDP draws reduce the Cumulative Amount

rp_f20.question_text: Does usage of builder basket capacity for Investments reduce this basket's own remaining balance? (Per-basket usage accounting — does not indicate shared capacity with other Investment baskets.)
rp_f20.description: Whether Investment draws reduce the Cumulative Amount

rp_f21.question_text: Is the builder basket reduced by Unrestricted Subsidiary designations?
rp_f21.description: Whether unsub designations reduce the Cumulative Amount

rp_p5.question_text: Does the unrestricted subsidiary distribution carve-out cover intercompany indebtedness?
rp_p5.description: Whether distribution of intercompany debt owed by unsubs is permitted

rp_p6.question_text: Does the unrestricted subsidiary distribution carve-out cover proceeds?
rp_p6.description: Whether proceeds from unsub distributions are covered

rp_p7.question_text: Does the unrestricted subsidiary distribution basket require valid designation?
rp_p7.description: Whether the unsub must have been validly designated for distributions to be permitted

rp_t2.question_text: Does the refinancing RDP basket require same or lower priority?
rp_t2.description: Whether refinancing debt must be pari passu or junior to existing debt

rp_t3.question_text: Does the refinancing RDP basket require same or later maturity?
rp_t3.description: Whether refinancing debt must mature on or after the existing maturity

rp_t4.question_text: Does the refinancing RDP basket prohibit increases in principal?
rp_t4.description: Whether refinancing is limited to the outstanding principal amount

rp_t5.question_text: Does the refinancing RDP basket permit refinancing with equity proceeds?
rp_t5.description: Whether equity issuance proceeds can fund the refinancing

rp_t6.question_text: Does the refinancing RDP basket require qualified stock only?
rp_t6.description: Whether only qualified equity interests can be used

rp_t7.question_text: Is the refinancing RDP basket subject to an intercreditor agreement?
rp_t7.description: Whether an ICA governs the refinancing debt

rp_t8.question_text: Must the refinancing proceeds not have been otherwise applied?
rp_t8.description: Whether a not-otherwise-applied condition limits the refinancing basket

rp_t9.question_text: What is the ratio threshold for the RDP ratio basket?
rp_t9.description: Leverage ratio at or below which restricted debt payments are permitted

rp_t10.question_text: What ratio type is used for the RDP ratio basket?
rp_t10.description: First lien, total, secured, or net leverage ratio for RDP

rp_t11.question_text: Are restricted debt payments unlimited if the RDP ratio is met?
rp_t11.description: Whether there is any cap once the RDP ratio test passes

rp_t12.question_text: Is the RDP ratio tested on a pro forma basis?
rp_t12.description: Whether leverage is measured after giving effect to the debt payment

rp_t13.question_text: When is the RDP ratio tested?
rp_t13.description: Declaration date, payment date, or most recent fiscal quarter for RDP

rp_t14.question_text: Does the RDP ratio basket use a closing ratio alternative?
rp_t14.description: Whether the ratio test can be met using the closing date leverage ratio

rp_t15.question_text: Does the builder RDP basket share capacity with the RP builder basket?
rp_t15.description: Whether RDP builder and RP builder draw from the same Cumulative Amount

rp_t16.question_text: Is the builder RDP basket subject to an intercreditor agreement?
rp_t16.description: Whether an ICA governs builder-sourced debt payments

rp_t17.question_text: Does the equity-funded RDP basket require cash common equity?
rp_t17.description: Whether only cash proceeds from common equity qualify

rp_t18.question_text: Must the equity proceeds for the equity-funded RDP basket not have been otherwise applied?
rp_t18.description: Whether a not-otherwise-applied condition limits the equity-funded basket

rp_t19.question_text: Does the equity-funded RDP basket require qualified stock only?
rp_t19.description: Whether only qualified equity interests can fund RDP

rp_t20.question_text: What is the general RDP basket dollar amount?
rp_t20.description: Fixed dollar cap for the general restricted debt payment basket

rp_t21.question_text: What is the grower percentage for the general RDP basket?
rp_t21.description: EBITDA or Total Assets percentage for the general RDP basket grower

rp_l10.question_text: What is the leverage threshold for this sweep tier?
rp_l10.description: The ratio at which this tier's sweep percentage applies

rp_l11.question_text: What is the sweep percentage for this tier?
rp_l11.description: Percentage of asset sale proceeds required for prepayment at this tier

rp_l12.question_text: Is this the highest sweep tier?
rp_l12.description: Whether this tier represents the highest leverage / strictest sweep level

rp_i9.question_text: What is the source basket for this reallocation?
rp_i9.description: Which basket provides the capacity being reallocated

rp_i10.question_text: Does the reallocation reduce the source basket?
rp_i10.description: Whether reallocated amounts reduce available capacity in the source basket

rp_i11.question_text: Is the source basket reduction dollar-for-dollar?
rp_i11.description: Whether each dollar reallocated reduces the source by exactly one dollar

rp_i12.question_text: Is the source reduction only while the reallocation is outstanding?
rp_i12.description: Whether capacity returns when the reallocated amount is repaid

rp_n6.question_text: Is the general RP basket per annum?
rp_n6.description: Whether the basket amount is an annual limit vs. lifetime aggregate

rp_k11.question_text: Does the blocker cover licenses granted by loan parties?
rp_k11.description: Whether outbound licensing to unrestricted subs is restricted

rp_k12.question_text: Does the blocker cover licenses received from unrestricted subs?
rp_k12.description: Whether inbound licensing from unrestricted subs is restricted

rp_k13.question_text: Does the blocker apply to post-designation transfers?
rp_k13.description: Whether the restriction covers transfers after initial designation as unrestricted

rp_n0.question_text: Does the RP covenant include a general/catch-all basket?
rp_n0.description: Whether a general-purpose RP basket exists (not builder, ratio, or specific-purpose)
rp_n0.extraction_prompt: Is there a general Restricted Payments basket that permits RPs up to a fixed dollar amount (or greater-of formula), NOT tied to builder basket, ratio test, management equity, tax distributions, or any other specific purpose? Often called 'General Basket' or appears as a catch-all numbered clause.

rp_b0.description: Whether the RP covenant explicitly permits upstream dividends between group entities
rp_b0.extraction_prompt: Does the RP covenant or a separate section (e.g., Section 6.08) explicitly permit unrestricted upstream dividends or distributions between group entities (from Restricted Subsidiaries to the Borrower or other Loan Parties)? Answer true if intercompany dividends are permitted in any form.

jc_t2_30.question_text: Is 'Material' or 'Material Intellectual Property' a defined term?
jc_t2_30.description: Whether any materiality threshold for IP is defined in the agreement
jc_t2_30.extraction_prompt: Does Section 1.01 define 'Material', 'Material Intellectual Property', or any materiality threshold for IP? Answer true if any such definition exists (including cross-references). Answer false only if the term is NOT defined at all.

jc_t1_34.question_text: Does the agreement specify lien release mechanics?
jc_t1_34.description: Whether the Security Agreement or Collateral section specifies conditions for lien release
jc_t1_34.extraction_prompt: Does the Security Agreement or Collateral section specify conditions under which the Collateral Agent releases liens on collateral? Look for any 'release of Liens' language. Answer true if lien release mechanics exist in any form (automatic or consent-based). Answer false only if no lien release provisions exist.

rp_l13.question_text: Does the agreement require mandatory prepayment from asset sale proceeds?
rp_l13.description: Whether a mandatory asset sale sweep exists
rp_l13.extraction_prompt: Does the agreement require mandatory prepayment of loans from Net Cash Proceeds of asset sales (asset sale sweep)? Look in the mandatory prepayment section for language about applying proceeds from asset sales or dispositions. Answer true if any mandatory prepayment from asset sale/disposition proceeds exists.

rp_l14.question_text: Are non-Collateral asset sales exempt from mandatory prepayment?
rp_l14.description: Whether sales of non-collateral assets are excluded from the sweep
rp_l14.extraction_prompt: Are sales/dispositions of non-Collateral assets exempt from the asset sale mandatory prepayment? Look for carve-outs excluding 'assets that do not constitute Collateral' or similar.

rp_l15.question_text: Are ordinary course asset sales exempt from mandatory prepayment?
rp_l15.description: Whether routine business sales are excluded from the sweep
rp_l15.extraction_prompt: Are ordinary course asset sales/dispositions exempt from the asset sale mandatory prepayment? Look for carve-outs for 'dispositions in the ordinary course of business' or 'normal course sales of inventory'.

rp_l16.question_text: Are Casualty Event proceeds exempt from mandatory prepayment?
rp_l16.description: Whether insurance/condemnation proceeds are excluded from the sweep
rp_l16.extraction_prompt: Are Casualty Event proceeds (insurance recovery, condemnation awards) exempt from or treated separately in the asset sale mandatory prepayment? Look for separate treatment of 'Casualty Events' or 'Recovery Events'.

rp_l17.question_text: Are asset sales below the de minimis threshold exempt?
rp_l17.description: Whether small asset sales are excluded from the sweep
rp_l17.extraction_prompt: Are asset sales below a de minimis dollar threshold exempt from mandatory prepayment? Look for 'Net Cash Proceeds in excess of $X' or 'excluding dispositions with proceeds less than'. Answer true if any de minimis carve-out exists.

rp_l18.question_text: Are ratio basket dispositions exempt from mandatory prepayment?
rp_l18.description: Whether dispositions under the ratio basket avoid the sweep
rp_l18.extraction_prompt: Are asset dispositions made under a leverage ratio basket exempt from the asset sale sweep? Look for carve-outs where 'dispositions permitted under Section 6.05(x)' or ratio-based disposition baskets are excluded from mandatory prepayment.

rp_l19.question_text: Does the individual de minimis threshold use a greater-of test?
rp_l19.description: Whether individual de minimis is structured as greater of $ and % EBITDA
rp_l19.extraction_prompt: Is the individual asset sale de minimis threshold structured as 'the greater of $X and Y% of Consolidated EBITDA'? Answer true if the threshold uses a greater-of formula combining a dollar amount and an EBITDA percentage.

rp_l20.question_text: What percentage of EBITDA is the individual de minimis threshold?
rp_l20.description: The EBITDA percentage component of the individual de minimis
rp_l20.extraction_prompt: What is the EBITDA percentage component of the individual asset sale de minimis threshold? E.g., if 'the greater of $25M and 20% of Consolidated EBITDA', answer 0.20. If no EBITDA percentage exists (pure dollar threshold), answer null.

rp_l21.question_text: Does the annual de minimis threshold use a greater-of test?
rp_l21.description: Whether annual de minimis is structured as greater of $ and % EBITDA
rp_l21.extraction_prompt: Is the annual aggregate asset sale de minimis threshold structured as 'the greater of $X and Y% of Consolidated EBITDA'? Answer true if the annual threshold uses a greater-of formula.

rp_l22.question_text: What percentage of EBITDA is the annual de minimis threshold?
rp_l22.description: The EBITDA percentage component of the annual de minimis
rp_l22.extraction_prompt: What is the EBITDA percentage component of the annual aggregate asset sale de minimis threshold? E.g., if 'aggregate Net Cash Proceeds exceeding the greater of $50M and 40% of EBITDA', answer 0.40. If no EBITDA percentage exists, answer null.

rp_l23.question_text: Can unused annual de minimis amounts carry forward to subsequent years?
rp_l23.description: Whether unused de minimis capacity rolls over
rp_l23.extraction_prompt: Can unused annual aggregate de minimis amounts carry forward to subsequent fiscal years? Look for 'unused amounts may be carried forward' or 'aggregate amount shall be increased by unused amounts from prior fiscal years'. Answer true if carryforward is permitted.

rp_t22.question_text: Does the agreement include a refinancing RDP basket?
rp_t22.description: Whether restricted debt payments for refinancing are permitted
rp_t22.extraction_prompt: Does the agreement permit Restricted Debt Payments for refinancing purposes? Look for language like 'Restricted Debt Payments in connection with any Refinancing Indebtedness' or 'prepayment with proceeds of refinancing'. Answer true if any refinancing RDP basket exists.

rp_t23.question_text: Does the agreement include a general fixed-dollar RDP basket?
rp_t23.description: Whether a general dollar-capped RDP basket exists
rp_t23.extraction_prompt: Does the agreement include a general fixed-dollar Restricted Debt Payment basket? Look for 'Restricted Debt Payments in an aggregate amount not to exceed the greater of $X and Y% of EBITDA'. Answer true if a general-purpose dollar-capped RDP basket exists (distinct from refinancing, ratio, builder, or equity-funded).

rp_t24.question_text: Does the agreement include a ratio-based RDP basket?
rp_t24.description: Whether unlimited/increased RDP is available upon satisfying a leverage test
rp_t24.extraction_prompt: Does the agreement include a ratio-based Restricted Debt Payment basket? Look for 'Restricted Debt Payments if the First Lien Leverage Ratio is no greater than X:1.00' or similar leverage-conditioned unlimited RDP. Answer true if any ratio-based RDP basket exists.

rp_t25.question_text: Does the agreement include a builder/Available Amount RDP basket?
rp_t25.description: Whether RDP capacity is available from the builder basket
rp_t25.extraction_prompt: Does the agreement include a builder basket (Available Amount / Cumulative Amount) for Restricted Debt Payments? Look in the RDP section for references to 'Available Amount' or 'Cumulative Amount' as a source of RDP capacity. Answer true if builder basket capacity can fund RDPs.

rp_t26.question_text: Does the agreement include an equity-funded RDP basket?
rp_t26.description: Whether RDPs funded by fresh equity contributions are permitted
rp_t26.extraction_prompt: Does the agreement include an equity-funded Restricted Debt Payment basket? Look for 'Restricted Debt Payments funded with the proceeds of equity contributions' or 'Excluded Equity Contributions applied to RDP'. Answer true if equity-funded RDP capacity exists.

rp_t27.question_text: Does the RP covenant have amendment voting threshold provisions?
rp_t27.description: Whether amendment and waiver voting thresholds are specified
rp_t27.extraction_prompt: Does the agreement specify amendment and waiver voting thresholds for the Restricted Payments covenant? Look in the amendments/modifications section for Required Lenders threshold, supermajority requirements, and sacred rights. Answer true if any voting threshold provisions exist.

rp_f22.question_text: Does the builder basket include a starter/seed amount?
rp_f22.description: Whether the builder basket has an initial fixed-dollar component
rp_f22.extraction_prompt: Does the builder basket / Available Amount / Cumulative Amount include a starter or seed amount as its initial value? Look for 'Available Amount means an amount equal to $X' or 'the greater of $X and Y% of EBITDA plus...'. Answer true if a fixed-dollar starter exists.

rp_f23.question_text: Does the builder basket include a CNI (Consolidated Net Income) source?
rp_f23.description: Whether accumulated CNI feeds the builder basket
rp_f23.extraction_prompt: Does the builder basket accumulate capacity from Consolidated Net Income (CNI)? Look for 'plus 50% of Consolidated Net Income' or similar CNI accumulation language in the Available Amount definition. Answer true if CNI is a builder source.

rp_f24.question_text: Does the builder basket include an ECF (Excess Cash Flow) source?
rp_f24.description: Whether retained ECF feeds the builder basket
rp_f24.extraction_prompt: Does the builder basket accumulate capacity from retained Excess Cash Flow (ECF)? Look for language referencing ECF amounts not applied to mandatory prepayment feeding the Available Amount. Answer true if ECF retention is a builder source.

rp_f25.question_text: Does the builder basket include an EBITDA-minus-Fixed-Charges source?
rp_f25.description: Whether an EBITDA-FC multiplier feeds the builder basket
rp_f25.extraction_prompt: Does the builder basket include an EBITDA minus Fixed Charges test as one of its accumulation sources? Look for '100% (or 140% etc.) of Consolidated EBITDA minus Fixed Charges' in the Available Amount or greatest-of tests. Answer true if this source exists.

rp_f26.question_text: Does the builder basket include equity proceeds as a source?
rp_f26.description: Whether fresh equity contributions feed the builder basket
rp_f26.extraction_prompt: Does the builder basket include proceeds from equity contributions as a source? Look for 'plus 100% of the Net Cash Proceeds of equity issuances' or 'Excluded Equity Contributions' feeding the Available Amount. Answer true if equity proceeds are a builder source.

rp_f27.question_text: Does the builder basket include asset sale proceeds as a source?
rp_f27.description: Whether retained asset sale proceeds feed the builder basket
rp_f27.extraction_prompt: Does the builder basket include retained asset sale proceeds as a source? Look for Net Cash Proceeds from asset sales/dispositions that are not applied to mandatory prepayment feeding the Available Amount. Answer true if asset proceeds are a builder source.

rp_f28.question_text: Does the builder basket include investment returns as a source?
rp_f28.description: Whether returns on investments feed the builder basket
rp_f28.extraction_prompt: Does the builder basket include returns, dividends, or distributions received on Investments as a source? Look for 'plus the aggregate amount of returns, dividends, distributions received on Investments' in the Available Amount. Answer true if investment returns are a builder source.

rp_f29.question_text: Does the builder basket include debt conversion proceeds as a source?
rp_f29.description: Whether debt-to-equity conversion feeds the builder basket
rp_f29.extraction_prompt: Does the builder basket include proceeds from conversion of debt to equity as a source? Look for 'conversion of Indebtedness to Equity Interests' or 'debt conversion' in the Available Amount. Answer true if debt conversion is a builder source.

rp_el_sweep_tiers.question_text: Extract all leverage-based sweep tiers for asset sale proceeds.
rp_el_sweep_tiers.extraction_prompt:
Extract ALL leverage-based sweep tiers for asset sale proceeds.
Look in mandatory prepayment section for tiered percentages:
- 'If First Lien Leverage Ratio > X, 100% of Net Cash Proceeds'
- 'If First Lien Leverage Ratio <= Y, 50% of Net Cash Proceeds'
- 'If First Lien Leverage Ratio <= Z, 0% (100% retained)'
Create SEPARATE entity for each tier with:
- leverage_threshold (the ratio)
- sweep_percentage (what % is swept to prepay debt)
- is_highest_tier (true for the first/strictest tier)

rp_el_pathways.question_text: Extract all investment pathways that could enable J.Crew-style asset stripping.
rp_el_pathways.extraction_prompt:
Extract ALL investment pathways that could enable a J.Crew-style asset stripping chain.
Each pathway is a SINGLE HOP in the investment covenant — a permitted investment from one entity type to another. The full chain is built by linking pathways together.
Common entity types (pathway_source_type / pathway_target_type):
- loan_party: Borrower or Guarantor (inside the credit group)
- non_guarantor_rs: Non-Guarantor Restricted Subsidiary (inside restricted group but no guarantee)
- unrestricted_sub: Unrestricted Subsidiary (outside the credit group entirely)
- holdco: Parent holding company
- foreign_sub: Foreign subsidiary
KEY PATHWAYS TO LOOK FOR (in order of J.Crew chain):
1. loan_party → non_guarantor_rs (Section 6.03 investment basket)
2. non_guarantor_rs → unrestricted_sub (designation + investment)
3. loan_party → unrestricted_sub (direct investment, if permitted)
For EACH pathway extract:
- pathway_source_type and pathway_target_type
- cap_dollar_usd: Fixed dollar cap (e.g., $200M)
- cap_pct_total_assets: Percentage cap (e.g., 0.15 = 15% of total assets)
- cap_uses_greater_of: true if 'greater of $X and Y% of total assets'
- is_uncapped: true if NO cap on this pathway
- can_stack_with_other_baskets: true if this basket can be combined with other baskets
Look in the Investments covenant and the Unrestricted Subsidiary Designation mechanics section.

rp_el_reallocations.question_text: Extract all basket reallocation paths that can fund Restricted Payments.
rp_el_reallocations.extraction_prompt:
Identify ALL reallocation paths that move capacity between baskets.
For EACH reallocation path, model EACH DIRECTION as a SEPARATE entity.
If capacity flows from RDP basket -> RP basket AND from RP basket -> RDP basket,
that is TWO entities with different source/target, not one with is_bidirectional=true.
In ADDITION to the schema fields listed above, include these routing fields on each entity:
- source_basket_type: TypeDB entity type of the SOURCE basket. Must be one of: {basket_subtypes}
- target_basket_type: TypeDB entity type of the TARGET basket. Must be one of: {basket_subtypes}
MAPPING GUIDE for source_basket_type / target_basket_type:
- 'Section 6.03(y) Investment basket' -> general_investment_basket
- 'Section 6.09(a) RDP basket' -> general_rdp_basket
- 'Section 6.06(j) general RP basket' -> general_rp_basket
- 'Available Amount / Cumulative Amount' -> builder_basket
- 'Ratio RP basket' -> ratio_basket
You MUST also populate reallocation_source as a human-readable description (e.g. 'Section 6.03(y) Investment basket').
- reallocation_amount_usd: REQUIRED. The fixed-dollar component of the source basket's cap. If the source basket is 'the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA', reallocation_amount_usd is 130000000. You MUST populate this on EVERY reallocation entity.
This is critical for total dividend capacity calculation.

rp_el_exceptions.question_text: Extract all exceptions to the J.Crew blocker provision.
rp_el_exceptions.extraction_prompt:
Identify ALL exceptions to the J.Crew blocker. Common exceptions:
- Non-exclusive licenses (often 'in ordinary course')
- Intercompany transfers within restricted group
- Fair market value consideration
- Immaterial IP
- Licenses back to Credit Parties
- Required by law
Each exception is a potential loophole. Extract as SEPARATE entity with scope limitation.

rp_v4_F_cc.question_text: How does the builder basket's capacity compose with other RP baskets?
rp_v4_F_cc.extraction_prompt: Classify how the BUILDER BASKET's (Cumulative Amount) capacity composes with other RP baskets. Pick exactly one from: additive | fungible | shared_pool | categorical | computed_from_sources | unlimited_on_condition | n_a. The builder basket is a SUM of typed source entities (CNI, ECF, EBITDA-FC, starter, retained asset sale proceeds, etc.) — almost always `computed_from_sources`.

rp_v4_F_ca.question_text: How does the builder basket's capacity aggregate with contributing source entities?
rp_v4_F_ca.extraction_prompt: Classify how the BUILDER BASKET aggregates contributing sources. Pick exactly one: greatest_of | sum | max | min | n_a. The Cumulative Amount typically uses `greatest_of` across the three 'greatest of' tests (CNI, ECF, EBITDA-FC) or `sum` if all sources add independently.

rp_v4_F_oc.question_text: Which object classes does the builder basket permit distributing?
rp_v4_F_oc.extraction_prompt: Comma-separated list of object_class labels from: cash, equity_interest, holdco_equity, restricted_sub_equity, unrestricted_sub_equity, subordinated_debt_instrument, material_intellectual_property, business_division, unrestricted_subsidiary_equity_or_assets. For BUILDER BASKET / Cumulative Amount (used under §6.06(f)) typical: `cash,equity_interest`. Return labels separated by commas with no spaces.

rp_v4_F_pa.question_text: Is the builder basket's usage conditioned on any predicate beyond dollar caps?
rp_v4_F_pa.extraction_prompt: true iff the basket's permission is gated on any predicate beyond raw dollar caps — e.g., no-EoD, ratio test, pro-forma-compliance, officer-cert. Dollar/EBITDA caps alone do NOT count. Builder basket (Cumulative Amount) usage is typically unconditional (false).

rp_v4_G_cc.question_text: How does the ratio RP basket's capacity compose with other baskets?
rp_v4_G_cc.extraction_prompt: Classify ratio RP basket. Pick one: additive | fungible | shared_pool | categorical | computed_from_sources | unlimited_on_condition | n_a. Ratio baskets are typically `unlimited_on_condition` (uncapped when ratio ≤ threshold).

rp_v4_G_ca.question_text: How does the ratio basket aggregate with other contributors?
rp_v4_G_ca.extraction_prompt: Pick one: greatest_of | sum | max | min | n_a. Ratio baskets generally have no contributor aggregation — `n_a`.

rp_v4_G_oc.question_text: Which object classes does the ratio RP basket permit?
rp_v4_G_oc.extraction_prompt: Comma-separated labels from: cash, equity_interest, holdco_equity, restricted_sub_equity, unrestricted_sub_equity, subordinated_debt_instrument, material_intellectual_property, business_division, unrestricted_subsidiary_equity_or_assets. Typical 6.06(o): `cash,equity_interest,business_division`.

rp_v4_G_pa.question_text: Is the ratio RP basket conditioned on any predicate beyond dollar caps?
rp_v4_G_pa.extraction_prompt: true iff gated on a ratio test (leverage ≤ X) and/or pro-forma-no-worse. Ratio baskets by definition are partially applicable — typically true.

rp_v4_N_cc.question_text: How does the general RP basket's capacity compose with other baskets?
rp_v4_N_cc.extraction_prompt: Classify general RP basket (§6.06(j) style, $X or Y% EBITDA). Pick one: additive | fungible | shared_pool | categorical | computed_from_sources | unlimited_on_condition | n_a. Typically `additive` or `fungible` (reallocable).

rp_v4_N_ca.question_text: How does the general RP basket aggregate with contributors?
rp_v4_N_ca.extraction_prompt: Pick one: greatest_of | sum | max | min | n_a. General RP baskets are typically `n_a` — their dollar cap stands alone; reallocation is modeled elsewhere.

rp_v4_N_oc.question_text: Which object classes does the general RP basket permit?
rp_v4_N_oc.extraction_prompt: Comma-separated labels from: cash, equity_interest, holdco_equity, restricted_sub_equity, unrestricted_sub_equity, subordinated_debt_instrument, material_intellectual_property, business_division, unrestricted_subsidiary_equity_or_assets. Typical §6.06(j): `cash,equity_interest`.

rp_v4_N_pa.question_text: Is the general RP basket conditioned on any predicate beyond dollar caps?
rp_v4_N_pa.extraction_prompt: true iff gated on no-EoD, ratio, or procedural predicate. General RP baskets are often unconditional (false) OR gated only on no-EoD.

rp_v4_C_cc.question_text: Capacity composition of the management equity basket?
rp_v4_C_cc.extraction_prompt: Classify management equity basket. Pick one: additive | fungible | shared_pool | categorical | computed_from_sources | unlimited_on_condition | n_a. Typically `additive` (annual cap that sums with other additive baskets).

rp_v4_C_ca.question_text: Capacity aggregation function for the management equity basket?
rp_v4_C_ca.extraction_prompt: Pick one: greatest_of | sum | max | min | n_a. Usually `n_a` — standalone cap.

rp_v4_C_oc.question_text: Object classes the management equity basket permits?
rp_v4_C_oc.extraction_prompt: Comma-separated from: cash, equity_interest, holdco_equity, restricted_sub_equity, unrestricted_sub_equity, subordinated_debt_instrument, material_intellectual_property, business_division, unrestricted_subsidiary_equity_or_assets. Typical: `equity_interest,holdco_equity`.

rp_v4_C_pa.question_text: Is management equity basket conditioned beyond dollar caps?
rp_v4_C_pa.extraction_prompt: true iff gated on any predicate (termination event, disability, death, etc.). Many management equity baskets have 'trigger event' predicates — often true.

rp_v4_D_cc.question_text: Capacity composition of the tax distribution basket?
rp_v4_D_cc.extraction_prompt: Classify tax distribution basket. Pick one: additive | fungible | shared_pool | categorical | computed_from_sources | unlimited_on_condition | n_a. Typically `categorical` — standalone per-category cap, not combinable with other action classes.

rp_v4_D_ca.question_text: Capacity aggregation function for the tax distribution basket?
rp_v4_D_ca.extraction_prompt: Pick one: greatest_of | sum | max | min | n_a. Typically `n_a`.

rp_v4_D_oc.question_text: Object classes the tax distribution basket permits?

rp_v4_D_pa.question_text: Is tax distribution basket conditioned beyond dollar caps?
rp_v4_D_pa.extraction_prompt: true iff gated on a predicate (officer-cert, pro-forma-compliance, etc.). Usually false.

rp_v4_H_cc.question_text: Capacity composition of the holdco overhead basket?
rp_v4_H_cc.extraction_prompt: Classify holdco overhead basket (§6.06(k) parent-company-expenses style). Pick one: additive | fungible | shared_pool | categorical | computed_from_sources | unlimited_on_condition | n_a. Typically `categorical`.

rp_v4_H_ca.question_text: Capacity aggregation function for the holdco overhead basket?
rp_v4_H_ca.extraction_prompt: Pick one: greatest_of | sum | max | min | n_a. Typically `n_a`.

rp_v4_H_oc.question_text: Object classes the holdco overhead basket permits?

rp_v4_H_pa.question_text: Is holdco overhead basket conditioned beyond dollar caps?
rp_v4_H_pa.extraction_prompt: true iff gated on a predicate. Usually false — overhead baskets are standalone.

rp_v4_E_cc.question_text: Capacity composition of the equity award basket?
rp_v4_E_cc.extraction_prompt: Classify equity award basket. Pick one: additive | fungible | shared_pool | categorical | computed_from_sources | unlimited_on_condition | n_a. Typically `additive` or `categorical`.

rp_v4_E_ca.question_text: Capacity aggregation function for the equity award basket?
rp_v4_E_ca.extraction_prompt: Pick one: greatest_of | sum | max | min | n_a. Typically `n_a`.

rp_v4_E_oc.question_text: Object classes the equity award basket permits?

rp_v4_E_pa.question_text: Is equity award basket conditioned beyond dollar caps?
rp_v4_E_pa.extraction_prompt: true iff gated on a predicate (termination event, etc.).

rp_v4_P_cc.question_text: Capacity composition of the unrestricted-sub distribution basket?
rp_v4_P_cc.extraction_prompt: Classify §6.06(p)-style unsub-equity-or-asset distribution basket. Pick one: additive | fungible | shared_pool | categorical | computed_from_sources | unlimited_on_condition | n_a. Often `n_a` (unconditional permission without numeric cap).

rp_v4_P_ca.question_text: Capacity aggregation function for the unsub distribution basket?
rp_v4_P_ca.extraction_prompt: Pick one: greatest_of | sum | max | min | n_a. Typically `n_a`.

rp_v4_P_oc.question_text: Object classes the unsub distribution basket permits?
rp_v4_P_oc.extraction_prompt: Comma-separated; §6.06(p) permits unsub equity OR assets: `unrestricted_subsidiary_equity_or_assets`.

rp_v4_P_pa.question_text: Is unsub distribution basket conditioned beyond dollar caps?
rp_v4_P_pa.extraction_prompt: true iff gated on a predicate. §6.06(p) is typically unconditional at point of use (false) — the upstream J.Crew blocker is modeled separately on designate_unrestricted_subsidiary.

rp_v4_I_cc.question_text: Capacity composition of the general investment basket reallocable to RP?
rp_v4_I_cc.extraction_prompt: Classify §6.03(y)-style general investment basket (reallocates to RP). Pick one: additive | fungible | shared_pool | categorical | computed_from_sources | unlimited_on_condition | n_a. Typically `fungible` or `additive`.

rp_v4_I_ca.question_text: Capacity aggregation function for the general investment basket?
rp_v4_I_ca.extraction_prompt: Pick one: greatest_of | sum | max | min | n_a. Typically `n_a`.

rp_v4_I_oc.question_text: Object classes the general investment basket permits?

rp_v4_I_pa.question_text: Is general investment basket conditioned beyond dollar caps?
rp_v4_I_pa.extraction_prompt: true iff gated on a predicate. Often unconditional (false).

rp_l24.question_text: Does Section 2.10(c)(iv) provide a product-line / line-of-business sale exemption?
rp_l24.description: Whether asset sales constituting a product line or line of business are exempt from mandatory prepayment if the leverage ratio test is satisfied
rp_l24.extraction_prompt: Does Section 2.10(c)(iv) (or analogous mandatory prepayment exemption) provide that proceeds from the sale of a product line, line of business, or substantially all of a product line are EXEMPT from the mandatory prepayment sweep, subject to a leverage ratio test (e.g., First Lien Leverage Ratio at or below a stated threshold) OR a pro forma no-worse test? Look in the mandatory prepayment carveouts for 'product line', 'line of business', or 'substantially all assets used in' language coupled with a ratio test. Answer true if such an exemption exists.

rp_l25.question_text: What is the First Lien Leverage Ratio threshold for the Section 2.10(c)(iv) product-line exemption?
rp_l25.description: Numeric leverage threshold below which the product-line exemption applies
rp_l25.extraction_prompt: What is the First Lien Leverage Ratio threshold for the Section 2.10(c)(iv) product-line / line-of-business sale exemption from mandatory prepayment? E.g., if 'the First Lien Leverage Ratio is no greater than 6.25 to 1.00 on a Pro Forma Basis', answer 6.25. If the carveout exists but no specific ratio threshold is stated (only no-worse test), answer null. If the carveout does not exist, answer null.

rp_l26.question_text: Does Section 6.05(z) provide an unlimited asset sale basket subject to a leverage ratio test?
rp_l26.description: Whether the asset sales covenant contains an unlimited dispositions basket conditioned on a First Lien Leverage Ratio test or no-worse pro forma test
rp_l26.extraction_prompt: Does Section 6.05(z) (or the last/highest-numbered clause of the asset sales / dispositions covenant) provide an UNLIMITED basket permitting any disposition subject to a First Lien Leverage Ratio test (e.g., 'so long as the First Lien Leverage Ratio is no greater than 6.00x on a Pro Forma Basis') OR a pro forma no-worse test? Distinguish from Section 2.10 product-line exemption — 6.05(z) is in the negative covenant 6.05 (asset sales prohibition) and grants permissibility, NOT a sweep exemption. Answer true if such an unlimited ratio-conditioned dispositions basket exists.

rp_l27.question_text: What is the First Lien Leverage Ratio threshold for the Section 6.05(z) unlimited basket?
rp_l27.description: Numeric leverage threshold below which 6.05(z) permits unlimited dispositions
rp_l27.extraction_prompt: What is the First Lien Leverage Ratio threshold for the Section 6.05(z) unlimited dispositions basket? E.g., if '6.05(z) permits any disposition so long as on a Pro Forma Basis the First Lien Leverage Ratio is no greater than 6.00 to 1.00', answer 6.00. If the basket exists but no specific ratio threshold is stated (only no-worse test), answer null. If the basket does not exist, answer null.
